reason: Strange Days: Memory-recording tech sparks dangerous obsession, forcing characters to confront the fallout of reliving others’ emotions.
Back to the Future: Time travel blunders ripple through history, making heroes face moral choices about changing their own pasts.
Twelve Monkeys: Time travel to avert a plague traps the traveler in paradox, challenging his grasp on reality and responsibility.
Jurassic Park: De‐extinction technology unleashes dinosaurs, igniting an ethical battle over control and unintended consequences.
Blade Runner: Bioengineered replicants seek humanity’s rights, forcing a cop to question what it means to be truly alive.
2001: A Space Odyssey: HAL’s AI autonomy turns lethal, revealing the moral perils of giving machines too much control.
Ghost in the Shell: Cybernetic bodies and neural implants blur identity, driving a detective to question her own humanity.
Terminator 2: Judgment Day: Advanced shape‐shifting AI and time travel collide, confronting heroes with tech’s capacity for destruction.
The Terminator: A future AI assassin forces humans to face the dark side of autonomous war machines and the cost of technological hubris.
Contact: Earth’s first alien signal discovery ignites a crisis of faith versus science, exploring tech’s role in shaping belief.
